description_long: This object is a large, two-handed sword with a straight, broad blade that appears to be forged from dark metal. The blade is rectangular with a flat tip and lacks any visible edge curvature, giving it a utilitarian, almost industrial appearance. The crossguard is wide and flat, extending horizontally to provide hand protection, and is also made of the same dark metal. The handle is cylindrical, tapering slightly towards the end, and features a rounded pommel for balance and grip security. The entire object has a matte, slightly rough finish, with no visible ornamentation or decorative elements. Its robust construction and lack of embellishments suggest it is designed for practical use rather than display.
description: A large, straight-bladed, two-handed sword with a wide crossguard and a simple, cylindrical handle, all in dark metal with a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the blade, crossguard, and the cylindrical handle with a rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the blade's flat, rectangular profile and the thinness of both the blade and crossguard.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the straight blade, wide crossguard, and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the blade's flatness and the handle's cylindrical shape.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, straight, dark metal, rounded pommel, no visible grip texture
